description: Chapter 8 - Time-independent perturbation theory. A guided structure for non-degenerate and degenerate expansions, van Vleck susceptibility and the Stark effect.

topics.title: Why perturbation theory is needed
topics.note: Approximating systems that differ weakly from a solvable model and identifying the regime where the expansion is useful.

topics.title: Hamiltonian, state and energy expansions
topics.note: Power series in the perturbation strength, equations order by order and normalization constraints.

topics.title: First-order non-degenerate corrections
topics.note: Diagonal energy shifts and the mixing of nearby unperturbed states through off-diagonal matrix elements.

topics.title: Second-order energy correction
topics.note: Virtual coupling through other levels, energy denominators and the sign and scale of quadratic shifts.

topics.title: Second-order state correction
topics.note: Normalization, direct mixing and paths through intermediate states in the corrected eigenvector.

topics.title: Exact matrix benchmark
topics.note: A solvable three-level model used to compare exact eigenvalues and vectors with their perturbative expansions.

topics.title: Why degeneracy changes the method
topics.note: Vanishing denominators, degenerate subspaces and complementary projection operators.

topics.title: First-order degenerate perturbation theory
topics.note: Diagonalizing the reduced perturbation inside a degenerate domain to obtain the correct zeroth-order combinations.

topics.title: Degenerate state and second-order corrections
topics.note: Mixing within and outside the split subspace and quadratic energy shifts from external states.

topics.title: van Vleck susceptibility
topics.note: From field-dependent energy corrections to magnetic moment, thermal magnetization and susceptibility.

topics.title: Anisotropic spin example
topics.note: A j = 3/2 spectrum in a transverse magnetic field, reduced perturbations and temperature-dependent response.

topics.title: Hydrogen atom in an electric field
topics.note: The Stark perturbation, the degenerate n = 2 manifold and the matrix elements of the position operator.

topics.title: Linear and quadratic Stark effects
topics.note: First-order splitting of mixed excited states and the second-order shift of the Hydrogen ground state.

topics.title: Stark selection rules
topics.note: Commutators, angular symmetry and the conditions delta m = 0 and delta l = plus or minus 1.

topics.title: Chapter synthesis: perturbative workflow
topics.note: A practical decision tree for choosing a basis, detecting degeneracy, computing corrections and interpreting observables.
